title: Strict positivity of the density / two-sided equivalence (ψ → −ψ uniformity)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Step E.** Equivalence (not just $T_\psi^*\mu\ll\mu$) requires $F_\psi>0$ $\mu$-a.s., equivalently $\mu\ll T_\psi^*\mu$.

**Gap:** Show the density $F_\psi=e^{X(\psi,\cdot)}$ is strictly positive a.s. and that the reverse absolute continuity holds. The natural route is $(T_\psi)^{-1}=T_{-\psi}$, so run Steps C–D for $-\psi$ to get the reciprocal density $F_{-\psi}$; but this requires the uniform integrability bound (see the core UI issue) to hold **simultaneously for both signs** of $\psi$. Must also rule out $T_\psi^*\mu$ charging a $\mu$-null set.

**Why nontrivial:** absolute continuity in one direction does not give equivalence; the cubic term changes sign under $\psi\to-\psi$, so the integrability argument must be symmetric. A cleaner packaging is via the one-parameter flow $\mu_t=T_{t\psi}^*\mu$, $t\in[0,1]$.
